title: add_group_member_capped uses BEGIN DEFERRED not BEGIN IMMEDIATE
description: store::add_group_member_capped uses pool.begin() which in SQLite defaults to BEGIN DEFERRED. Two concurrent callers can both acquire read locks, both read count < cap, then both attempt to promote to write locks. One will succeed; the other will get SQLITE_BUSY. The count + insert is not safe without BEGIN IMMEDIATE. Same pattern as nie-kef6.29 (already filed) — confirming the same root cause is present after round 20 changes.
